h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought it was just outside the rules because people correct it so fast.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think I made the corrections count as the rules there
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to simply answer the tutor's question about when the phrase sounds off, continuing their tentative exploration of the example.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought significance was enough to call it an important improvement.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to directly answer what evidence on the poster would license an important improvement.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought the label should say a canyon came from one huge flood, or something like that.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: make me think the small wearing and carrying matters
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was moving forward with a compromise label that combined floods and gradual erosion without examining the bias of their starting point.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought “presumed innocent” meant the court was treating it as true that the accused did not do it, unless someone proved otherwise.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: and I think that was where I framed it wrongly.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue to express confusion about why 'not guilty' sounds like 'did not do it' without identifying the underlying conceptual error.
The learner was expected to directly answer the tutor's question about how long the herd can remain healthy as winter browse shrinks.

scored.recoheredEarlier: My first instinct was that once the beaker looks clear, the salt has gone, so I thought the mass might drop.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to provide a direct prediction of whether the balance reading would fall, stay put, or rise.
